title: GraphMAE Self-Supervised Graph Lab
summary: Create a self-supervised graph lab for pretraining, downstream transfer, and graph representation analysis with polished demos.
knowledge_base: GraphMAE Self-Supervised Graph Lab is positioned as a Self-Supervised Graph Learning starter that emphasizes fast iteration, clear documentation, and production-friendly interfaces.
The primary value proposition is: Create a self-supervised graph lab for pretraining, downstream transfer, and graph representation analysis with polished demos.
Recommended operating model: ship a small API, add observability, validate outputs with stakeholder review, and monitor the top business KPI every week.
Typical stakeholders include analytics, operations, product, and leadership teams working on graphmae, graph, self-supervised, gnn.
Source paper: GraphMAE: Self-Supervised Masked Graph Autoencoders

Production differentiation focus: Turn upstream self-supervised graph learning capabilities into a production-style application with operations-facing workflows.; Add deployment packaging, monitoring hooks, and screenshot-ready demos instead of notebook-only output.; Connect model behavior to concrete business decisions so the project is easier to evaluate and present.
why_high_score: Official or primary upstream repo with 585 GitHub stars.

GraphMAE: Self-Supervised Masked Graph Autoencoders in KDD'22
differentiation_hooks: Turn upstream self-supervised graph learning capabilities into a production-style application with operations-facing workflows.
Add deployment packaging, monitoring hooks, and screenshot-ready demos instead of notebook-only output.
